summary: AIR-DROW Calibration Reality & QA Edition: four-point hand calibration now captures only the requested targets, persists immediately across projects, and uses real browser probes for network and local readiness states.
features: Locks Android text selection, long-press Copy/Search/Translate callouts and blue tap-highlight flashes across onboarding, overlays and the studio while preserving real editable fields.
Uses target-confirmed four-point hand calibration: off-target or unstable hands cannot advance the sequence, and malformed geometry exits safely instead of repeating.
Saves a completed calibration immediately to dedicated local browser storage and to the active project through serialized writes, then applies it to every hand-drawing session.
Uses same-origin reachability, storage write/read and local runtime checks instead of optimistic ready or online labels.
Removes the stale upstream MediaPipe source-map directive from production output so DevTools no longer requests a missing .map file.
Keeps the explicit Hand check passed confirmation visible before the scanner fades away with a calm completion animation, with reliable inline vector warning and verified marks.

privacy.drawings: Your drawing data and preferences stay on your device unless you choose to export, share, or use an optional AI creation action.
privacy.camera: Camera permission is requested only after you choose to open the camera. Camera video is not saved by AIR-DROW.
privacy.handTracking: The deployed app uses the local same-origin hand model and runtime. It does not download a hand model while you draw.
privacy.checks: App readiness and Hand drawing check only read local browser and app state. They do not open the camera themselves or send a report anywhere.

buildPolicy.runtimeNetwork: The deployed browser loads the hand runtime and hand model from the same AIR-DROW site.
buildPolicy.delivery: The complete source package includes the verified hand model. The production build validates the local model before publishing.
